mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declining-rate periods are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that triggers rapid equity repricing of recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5 near cycle lows leaves limited room for further declines
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative mean 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears when tested out-of-sample across two distinct decades.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that reinforces corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon only when both series rise together; neither series alone reliably predicts returns.
failure_condition: The joint-signal Rank IC is not meaningfully higher than the best single-series IC (RSAFS-only or HOUST-only) over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth signals capacity constraints and margin compression risk that precede equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the same deceleration in early-cycle regimes carries no predictive power because it reflects normalization rather than distress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.677 reflects an already elevated production base
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly more negative in late-cycle periods than in early/mid-cycle periods (interaction test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS over a 6-month trailing window reflects durable labor income generation that supports consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as a slow-moving trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year window, or reverses sign in more than one distinct macro cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising while CPIAUCSL inflation is simultaneously elevated above trend, markets price in an aggressive tightening path that compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days; neither rising rates nor elevated inflation alone triggers the effect absent the other.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL trigger fails to produce mean 20-day forward returns significantly below the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth signals confirmation of a durable expansion that reduces uncertainty premia in equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in mature expansions the same growth print carries diminished informational value because it is already priced.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following positive GDPC1 prints are not significantly higher in recovery-regime subsamples than in mature-expansion subsamples (difference-in-means p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that prompts short-horizon risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return following triggered INDPRO drops is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across at least three historical occurrences, or reverses sign out-of-sample.
